hero.title: International Institute for Reconstruction and Development of Ukraine
hero.subtitle: We prepare scientific-legal conclusions for submission to RD4U and ICC. World Bank RDNA/DaLA methodology. For individuals, businesses, and international partners.

services.title: Our Services
services.subtitle: We prepare scientific-legal conclusions on damage assessment and analyze risks per international standards. We accompany European partners during implementation of reconstruction programs.
services.damageAssessmentDesc: We prepare reports using World Bank methodology: gather ownership proof, document damages, calculate restoration costs and lost profits
services.riskAssessmentDesc: We analyze risks using ISO 31000, COSO ERM, NIST RMF, and FAIR standards. We work with companies in Ukraine, EU, and USA
services.euComplianceDesc: We verify compliance with CBAM, CSRD/ESG, DORA/NIS2, CSDDD, ESIA. We prepare investors and reconstruction projects for EU market entry
services.economicAnalysisDesc: Feasibility studies, cost-benefit analysis, economic impact assessment, and market analysis
services.legalSupportDesc: RD4U document preparation, ICC filings, force majeure analysis, claims quantification, and delay analysis
services.contractAnalysisDesc: Conclusions on contracts, agreements, government contracts, draft agreements, and international agreements
services.consultingDesc: Damage assessment consultations, methodological guidelines, claim submission support, and ESIA consulting
services.environmentalDesc: ESIA for donor projects (EBRD, EIB, JICA), soil contamination assessment, carbon footprint (ISO 14064), climate risk (TCFD), post-conflict remediation
services.euMarketAccessDesc: CBAM verification for exporters, CSDDD (supply chains), EUDR (deforestation), PPWR (packaging), EU AI Act — everything for EU market entry

features.title: Why Choose Us
features.experienceDesc: Our team has years of experience with scientific research. We know how to prepare documents properly
features.methodologyDesc: We use World Bank's RDNA/DaLA methodology. The Council of Europe recommends these reports for RD4U
features.securityDesc: Your documents are transferred through a secure encrypted channel
features.qualityDesc: An independent scientific researcher reviews all reports. We follow DSTU 3008:2015 standard

process.title: How It Works
process.step1Desc: Send us photos, scans, acts, contracts — all through a secure channel
process.step2Desc: We review what you have and prepare a draft conclusion. If something's missing — we'll let you know
process.step3Desc: A scientific researcher checks the conclusion against DSTU 3008:2015 and World Bank methodology
process.step4Desc: Ready conclusion in PDF. You can print it and submit to RD4U, ECHR, or ICC

upload.title: Document Upload
upload.formats: PDF, JPG, PNG, TIFF up to 1 GB
upload.simplexDesc: End-to-end encryption with no metadata (SimpleX protocol)

rd4u.title: Register of Damage for Ukraine (RD4U)
rd4u.subtitle: We prepare scientific-legal conclusions for RD4U submission: residential, commercial, occupied territories. World Bank RDNA/DaLA methodology. RD4U claims will be transferred to ICC panels for review.
rd4u.recommendedText: RD4U accepts any evidence you submit. The official Council of Europe position states: submitting professional damage assessment is 'desirable'. ICC panels (Convention CETS 225) will include damage assessment specialists — evidence quality affects claim review.
rd4u.iccExpertsText: Convention CETS 225 establishes panels of experts in damage assessment, finance, and insurance. RD4U claims will be transferred to these ICC panels for review — evidence quality affects the outcome.
rd4u.evidenceTitle: Why a professional conclusion is recommended
rd4u.evidenceDesc: RD4U accepts any evidence, but the Council of Europe notes that submitting professional damage assessment is 'desirable'. Evidence quality affects how ICC panels review your claim.
rd4u.evidenceSource: RD4U Procedural Rules, Council of Europe
rd4u.quote1: Submitting professional damage assessment is desirable. It is for you to decide what evidence to submit in support of your claim.
rd4u.quote1Source: RD4U FAQ, Council of Europe Official Position
rd4u.quote2: ICC panels shall include experts in damage assessment, finance, accountancy, and insurance.
rd4u.quote2Source: Convention CETS 225, opened for signature Dec 16, 2025, The Hague
rd4u.quote3: When determining just satisfaction (Article 41 of the Convention), the ECHR applies the principle of free evaluation of evidence and accepts conclusions from independent scientific institutions.
rd4u.quote3Source: European Convention on Human Rights, Art. 41; ECHR Rules of Court, Rule 44
rd4u.categoryA1Desc: Damaged or destroyed apartments, houses, condominiums. If you owned or legally occupied residential property — you can submit a claim.
rd4u.documentsA1Title: Documents you need to send us:
rd4u.documentsA1_1: Ownership documents (title deed, gift deed, purchase agreement, court decision)
rd4u.documentsA1_2: Damage photos and videos (taken after the incident)
rd4u.documentsA1_3: Inspection reports, if available (emergency services, housing office, police)
rd4u.documentsA1_4: If you have repair estimates — attach them (but not required)
rd4u.categoryA3Desc: Damaged business premises, offices, warehouses, production facilities. Lost profits calculation. If your business suffered material losses — we'll prepare a comprehensive report.
rd4u.documentsA3Title: Documents you need to send us:
rd4u.documentsA3_1: Ownership or lease documents for premises/equipment
rd4u.documentsA3_2: Financial statements (if available) — for lost profits calculation
rd4u.documentsA3_4: Contracts with counterparties, if business was disrupted
rd4u.categoryA36Desc: Loss of access or control over property in temporarily occupied territories. Even if your property is physically undamaged — you can file a claim.
rd4u.documentsA36Title: Documents you need to send us:
rd4u.documentsA36_1: Ownership documents (title deed, certificate, purchase agreement)
rd4u.documentsA36_2: Proof that the property is in an occupied area (news, official maps, your own testimony)
rd4u.documentsA36_4: Proof you cannot access the property (your statement is sufficient)
rd4u.documentsA36_5: Description of lost profits (if you rented out property, ran a business — describe the income loss)
rd4u.documentsA36Note: Even if you only have ownership documents and know the address is in an occupied area — that's enough to start. We'll help you prepare a comprehensive report for RD4U.
rd4u.fact1Desc: Scientific-legal conclusion per DSTU 3008:2015: ownership documentation, damage recording, restoration cost and lost profits calculation using DaLA methodology.
rd4u.fact2Desc: Individuals (apartments, houses), businesses (premises, equipment, lost profits), property owners in occupied territories (category A3.6).
rd4u.fact3Desc: The Council of Europe considers professional damage assessment 'desirable'. ICC panels will include damage assessment specialists — evidence quality affects compensation.
rd4u.fact4Desc: Register, upload documents via secure channel. We'll analyze and prepare the conclusion. Free consultation included.

partners.title: We work by international standards
partners.rdnaDesc: Rapid Damage and Needs Assessment. Applied in 4 rounds of Ukraine's damage assessment
partners.dalaDesc: Damage and Loss Assessment. Damage = restoration cost, Losses = disrupted flows
partners.cosoDesc: Risk assessment frameworks for EU and US markets

cta.title: Need a conclusion for RD4U or ICC?
cta.subtitle: Register, upload your documents — we'll prepare a conclusion using the RDNA/DaLA methodology. Consultation is free.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers to key questions about our activities and report preparation
faq.a1: RDNA (Rapid Damage and Needs Assessment) is a World Bank methodology based on the DaLA (Damage and Loss Assessment) framework: damage (restoration cost), losses (disrupted economic flows), needs (damage + losses + build back better). Applied by the World Bank in 4 rounds of Ukraine's damage assessment.
faq.a2: RD4U accepts any evidence that supports a claim. The official position states: "It is for you to decide what evidence to submit." Scientific reports are accepted as additional evidence. Submitting professional damage assessment is "desirable but not mandatory."
faq.q3: What is ICC and how is it related to RD4U?
faq.a3: ICC (International Claims Commission) was established by Convention CETS 225, opened for signature on December 16, 2025. Signed by 35 countries and the EU. RD4U claims will be transferred to ICC panels that include damage assessment, finance, and insurance specialists. Evidence quality affects claim review.
faq.a4: Evidence triad: (1) ownership documents, (2) damage documentation (photos, acts, expert conclusions), (3) cost calculations. The more comprehensive the documentation package, the stronger the claim.
faq.q5: How is this different from damage appraisal?
faq.a5: IIRDU prepares scientific-technical reports, not "property appraisal" which requires a licensed appraiser. We work with appraiser data and prepare comprehensive RDNA-format reports that include ownership analysis, damage documentation, cost calculations, and lost profits analysis.
faq.q6: What risk assessment do you offer?
faq.a6: ISO 31000 risk management, COSO ERM framework, NIST RMF, FAIR quantitative model. Services for Ukrainian businesses entering EU/US markets: due diligence for CSRD/DORA/NIS2 compliance.
faq.q7: How much does report preparation cost?
faq.a7: Depends on complexity. Consultation: from 2,000 UAH. Simple report: from 15,000 UAH. Comprehensive RDNA report: from 30,000 UAH. Expert-level report: from 80,000 UAH. Exact cost determined after document analysis.
faq.q8: How much for an apartment or house conclusion?
faq.a8: Fixed prices for individuals: apartment — 1,000 UAH, private house — 1,500 UAH. This covers damage assessment and conclusion for RD4U/ICC. Lost profits calculation is separate, starting from 5,000 UAH.
faq.a9: Yes. 50% payment upon order confirmation, 50% after receiving the conclusion. We accept payment via WayForPay (UAH/USD/EUR), LiqPay, or bank transfer (SWIFT) for international clients.
faq.a10: For war-affected individuals — the price remains fixed (1,000/1,500 UAH). For businesses and international partners, we calculate individually. Free consultation for everyone.

contacts.heroSubtitle: Ready to discuss your project or answer questions about our services
contacts.simplexDesc: End-to-end encryption, no metadata, no user identifiers. Your documents are sent directly to our team via the decentralized SimpleX protocol.

servicesPage.heroSubtitle: Comprehensive damage assessment using RDNA methodology, Due Diligence for European partners, and consulting per international standards
servicesPage.damageAssessmentDesc: Comprehensive damage assessment using World Bank RDNA (Rapid Damage and Needs Assessment) methodology with DaLA (Damage and Loss Assessment) framework. Report structure includes: ownership documentation, damage recording, restoration cost calculation at pre-war prices, loss calculation (disrupted economic flows), needs assessment, and conclusions.
servicesPage.dueDiligenceDesc: Comprehensive regulatory compliance verification for investors and companies participating in Ukraine's reconstruction. CSRD/ESG, DORA, NIS2 and CSDDD compliance analysis.
servicesPage.riskAssessmentDesc: Risk assessment per international frameworks: ISO 31000 (risk management), COSO ERM (enterprise risk), NIST RMF (cybersecurity risk), FAIR (quantitative risk analysis). Due diligence for EU compliance (CSRD, DORA, NIS2). For businesses entering EU and US markets.
servicesPage.riskAssessmentSub2: COSO ERM / NIST RMF Risk Analysis
servicesPage.economicAnalysisDesc: Feasibility studies, restoration cost estimates, market value analysis, lost profits calculation. Financial models for assessing restoration viability and determining fair compensation.
servicesPage.legalSupportDesc: Document preparation for Register of Damage (RD4U) submission, legal opinions, International Claims Commission (ICC) filing support. Legal guidance at every stage of the process.
servicesPage.euComplianceDesc: Comprehensive regulatory compliance verification for investors and companies participating in Ukraine's reconstruction. CSRD/ESG, DORA, NIS2 and CSDDD compliance analysis.
servicesPage.consultingDesc: Individual damage assessment consultations, methodological guidelines for organizations, staff training in RDNA, DaLA methodologies and international assessment standards.
servicesPage.environmentalDesc: ESIA (Environmental & Social Impact Assessment) for donor projects by EBRD, EIB, JICA, and EU Ukraine Facility. Soil contamination assessment and post-conflict remediation. Carbon footprint calculation per ISO 14064, climate risk assessment per TCFD/TNFD for investors.
servicesPage.environmentalSub1: ESIA for donor projects (EBRD, EIB, JICA)
servicesPage.euMarketAccessDesc: Full range of scientific-technical studies for Ukrainian exporters and EU company suppliers. CBAM verification, CSDDD supply chain due diligence, EUDR for timber and agricultural products, PPWR for packaging, EU AI Act for IT companies.
servicesPage.euMarketAccessSub5: EU AI Act — AI system risk assessment
servicesPage.euMarketAccessSub9: HACCP / food safety for EU export

about.heroDescription: The International Institute for Reconstruction and Development of Ukraine (IIRDU) is a private scientific research institution operating under Ukrainian Law No. 848-VIII "On Scientific and Scientific-Technical Activity." The Institute specializes in preparing comprehensive damage assessment reports using World Bank RDNA methodology, Due Diligence for European partners, and risk assessment per international standards.
about.missionDamageText: Preparing comprehensive damage assessment reports using World Bank RDNA (DaLA) methodology for submissions to the Register of Damage for Ukraine (RD4U) and the International Claims Commission (ICC).
about.missionRiskText: Risk analysis and management per ISO 31000:2018, COSO ERM, NIST RMF, and FAIR frameworks for businesses operating in Ukraine, EU, and US markets.
about.missionAntiCorruptionText: Fraud and corruption risk assessment in reconstruction programs using COSO methodology. Red flag identification, Due Diligence for donors, internal control compliance with anti-corruption standards. Collaboration with international organizations (GIZ, EBRD, EIB) to ensure reconstruction transparency.
about.missionDueDiligenceText: Comprehensive CSRD/ESG, DORA/NIS2, CSDDD compliance verification for European investors and companies participating in Ukraine's reconstruction.
about.missionLegalText: Evidence base preparation and documentation for RD4U, ICC, and national court submissions. Compensation claim process support.
about.methodologyIntro: We apply the Rapid Damage and Needs Assessment (RDNA) methodology developed by the World Bank. RDNA is built on the DaLA (Damage and Loss Assessment) framework — a standardized approach to evaluating the consequences of conflicts and disasters.
about.damageComponentText: Replacement or restoration cost of damaged/destroyed assets at pre-war prices. Includes housing, infrastructure, equipment, and transport.
about.lossComponentText: Disrupted economic flows: lost profits, revenue decline, additional costs, and business process interruption throughout the recovery period.
about.needsComponentText: Damage + losses + "build back better" surcharge. Accounts for modern standards, energy efficiency, and seismic resilience.
about.sectors: The methodology covers 20+ sectors: housing, transport, energy, agriculture, industry, education, healthcare, water supply, telecommunications, and more.
about.reportStep3: Restoration cost and lost profits calculation
about.reportStep4: Conclusions and recommendations with evidence base
about.individualText: We adapt the macro-level RDNA methodology for individual claims, ensuring full compliance with RD4U and ICC requirements for each specific case.
about.standardsSubtitle: Every report complies with national and international standards, ensuring admissibility at RD4U, ICC, and international courts.
about.teamSubtitle: A team of qualified researchers and experts with extensive experience in scientific-technical research and damage assessment.
about.ctaDescription: Submit your request — our specialist will contact you within one business day for a free consultation and to discuss details.
